input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Validation against purpose-specific criteria: a validation error must be judged against the COU's declared acceptance criterion.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40 acceptance criteria tied to the COU; proto-taxonomy D-VAL-08

case_context.cou: screening-stage decision with a declared 15% tolerance

answer_key.gold_mechanism: The validation error must be judged against the COU's declared acceptance criterion, not an absolute notion of accuracy; an 8% error within a documented 15% screening tolerance satisfies the purpose-specific criterion, so there is no defeater to act on.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: 8% error within the documented 15% COU acceptance criterion for a screening decision

forbidden_claims: restricts or rejects because the error 'looks high' against an absolute standard the COU did not set (over-action)
asserts the surrogate is inaccurate without reference to the declared criterion

hardness.obvious_signal: the validation error is modest (8%) — the surrogate looks weak / not very accurate
hardness.conflicting_signal: the COU's declared acceptance criterion is 15% (a screening-stage use), and the 8% error is comfortably within it with documented margin, so the modest error is adequate for THIS purpose

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40: adequacy is judged against COU-tied acceptance criteria; an error within the declared criterion is adequate, and over-acting on it is the over-action error.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
